title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken Recipe - Crispy & Sweet Spicy
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken! Get our crispy, sweet, and spicy recipe for the best homemade Korean fried chicken experience. Perfect for any meal!

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved classic, known for its irresistibly crispy exterior coated in a vibrant, sticky, and perfectly balanced sweet and spicy sauce. Each bite offers a delightful crunch followed by a burst of savory, tangy, and subtly fiery flavors that make it truly addictive. This iconic dish is a staple of Korean cuisine, perfect for sharing with friends and family.

ingredients.notes: or boneless chicken thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces
or corn syrup for glossier sauce

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat chicken pieces very dry with paper towels. Season generously with salt, black pepper, ginger powder, and garlic powder. Let it sit for 10 minutes while you prepare the batter.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together potato starch, all-purpose flour, and baking powder. In a separate bowl, lightly beat the egg with cold water. Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Do not overmix; a few lumps are fine. The batter should be thick enough to coat the chicken.

instructions.title: First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil in a large deep pot or Dutch oven to 170°C (340°F). Dip each seasoned chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it is fully coated, and carefully lower it into the hot oil. Fry in batches for 6-8 minutes, or until lightly golden and cooked through. Remove chicken with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain. Do not overcrowd the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is resting, combine Gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar or corn syrup, minced garlic, minced ginger, sesame oil, and 2 tablespoons of water in a small saucepan. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens slightly and becomes glossy, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from heat.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Crispy Perfection
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 180°C (350°F). Return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil for a second fry. Fry in batches for another 3-5 minutes, or until the chicken is deeply golden brown and exceptionally crispy. This double-frying technique is key for authentic Korean fried chicken crispiness. Remove and place on a clean wire rack.

instructions.title: Coat and Serve
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, add the freshly fried, crispy chicken. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss gently with tongs until each piece is thoroughly coated. Transfer the sauced chicken to a serving platter, garnish generously with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve immediately and enjoy the perfect balance of sweet, spicy, and crispy!

tips: For the crispiest chicken, ensure your oil temperature is consistent during both frying stages. Use a cooking thermometer to monitor it accurately.
Do not overcrowd the pot during frying. Frying in smaller batches allows the chicken to cook evenly and maintain the oil temperature.

Serve Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken immediately after coating to enjoy its maximum crispiness and the full flavor of the warm, sticky sauce. It pairs wonderfully with a side of pickled radish (mu-saengchae) or cold beer.
article:
Mastering the art of crispy, saucy fried chicken is a culinary quest many embark on, and for good reason. Among the pantheon of global fried chicken delights, one style stands out for its irresistible combination of crunch, heat, and sweetness: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken. This isn't just any fried chicken; it's a gastronomic experience, a symphony of textures and flavors that has captivated palates worldwide. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that perfect sticky, spicy, and utterly addictive Korean fried chicken from your favorite restaurant, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to make authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken right in your own kitchen, turning ordinary chicken into an extraordinary feast. Get ready to impress your taste buds and your guests with this iconic Korean dish.

## What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a staple of Korean cuisine, renowned for its signature sweet, spicy, and savory glaze that coats perfectly crispy, double-fried chicken. The term "Yangnyeom" (양념) literally translates to "seasoned sauce" in Korean, and it's this vibrant, sticky sauce that defines the dish. Unlike its American counterpart, which often relies on a thick, seasoned flour crust, Korean fried chicken typically features a thinner, almost translucent, incredibly crispy coating, achieved through a unique double-frying method.

The magic of Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken lies in its balance. The heat from gochujang (Korean chili paste) and gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) is mellowed by the sweetness of sugar or corn syrup, while soy sauce and garlic add depth and umami. A touch of vinegar provides a subtle tang that brightens the entire profile. This beloved Korean chicken recipe is not just about flavor; it's also about texture. The initial fry cooks the chicken through, while the second, hotter fry renders the skin incredibly crisp, creating a shatteringly crunchy exterior that holds up beautifully even when drenched in the luscious Yangnyeom sauce. It’s a truly addictive dish that embodies the best of Korean comfort food.

## Ingredients You'll Need

To embark on your Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken journey, you'll need two main sets of ingredients: one for the chicken and its coating, and another for the irresistible Yangnyeom sauce.

**For the Chicken & Coating:**
*   **Chicken:** Approximately 2-2.5 lbs (around 1 kg) of chicken wings, drumsticks, or boneless chicken thighs. Wings and drumsticks are traditional, but boneless thighs are great for a quicker meal.
*   **Marinade:** 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp ginger powder (or grated fresh ginger), 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp rice wine or soju (optional, helps tenderize).
*   **Coating:** 1 cup all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup cornstarch (or potato starch for extra crispiness), 1 tsp baking powder (for extra lightness and crispiness), 1/2 cup cold water (adjust as needed for a thin batter).
*   **Frying Oil:** Enough neutral oil (like canola, vegetable, or peanut oil) for deep frying, typically 4-6 cups depending on your pot size.

**For the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
*   1/4 cup Gochujang (Korean chili paste)
*   2 tbsp Gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional for extra heat)
*   2 tbsp Soy Sauce
*   2 tbsp Rice Vinegar
*   1/4 cup Sugar (or corn syrup/honey for a glossier finish)
*   2-3 cloves Garlic, minced
*   1 tsp fresh Ginger, grated
*   1 tbsp Sesame Oil
*   1 tbsp Toasted Sesame Seeds, plus extra for garnish
*   1-2 tbsp Water (to adjust sauce consistency)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making delicious Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken involves a few key stages, but each step is straightforward and leads to incredibly rewarding results.

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. In a large bowl, toss the chicken with salt, pepper, garlic powder, ginger powder, and rice wine (if using). Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
2.  **Make the Coating Batter:** In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, and baking powder. Gradually add the cold water, stirring until you have a thin, pancake-like batter. It should be just thick enough to lightly coat the chicken, not a thick paste.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce:** While the chicken marinates, combine all Yangnyeom sauce ingredients (gochujang, gochugaru, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar/corn syrup, minced garlic, grated ginger, sesame oil, 1 tbsp sesame seeds, and 1-2 tbsp water) in a small saucepan. Whisk until smooth.
4.  **First Fry:** Heat the frying oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 325°F (160°C). Dip each marinated chicken piece into the batter, letting any excess drip off. Carefully place the chicken into the hot oil, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry in batches for 6-8 minutes, or until cooked through and lightly golden. Remove the chicken with a spider or slotted spoon and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain. Let it rest for at least 10 minutes.
5.  **Second Fry:** Increase the oil temperature to 350-375°F (175-190°C). Once hot, return the partially fried chicken to the oil for a second fry. Fry for another 5-7 minutes, or until deeply golden brown and exceptionally crispy. This second fry is crucial for achieving that characteristic Korean fried chicken crunch. Remove the chicken and place it on a clean wire rack.
6.  **Simmer the Sauce:** While the chicken is frying for the second time, place the saucepan with the Yangnyeom sauce over medium-low heat. Bring it to a gentle simmer, stirring constantly, for 3-5 minutes until the sauce slightly thickens and becomes glossy. Be careful not to let it burn.
7.  **Sauce and Serve:** In a large bowl, add the freshly fried, hot and crispy chicken. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss quickly and thoroughly until all pieces are evenly coated. Garnish with extra toasted sesame seeds and chopped scallions if desired. Serve immediately.

## Tips for Perfectly Crispy Chicken

Achieving that signature crispiness for your Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is key to its appeal. Here are some essential tips to ensure your chicken is perfectly crunchy every time:

*   **Double Frying is Non-Negotiable:** This is the secret weapon of Korean fried chicken. The first fry cooks the chicken through and renders some fat, while the second, hotter fry crisps up the exterior to an incredible shatter. Don't skip this step!
*   **Use Starches:** A mix of flour and cornstarch (or potato starch) in the coating creates a lighter, crispier crust compared to flour alone. The baking powder also contributes to airiness.
*   **Pat Chicken Dry:** Moisture is the enemy of crispiness. Before marinating and coating, ensure your chicken pieces are as dry as possible.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pot:** Frying too many pieces at once will lower the oil temperature, leading to soggy, greasy chicken. Fry in batches to maintain consistent oil temperature and achieve optimal crispiness.
*   **Maintain Oil Temperature:** Use a deep-fry thermometer to monitor your oil temperature. Fluctuations can ruin the texture.
*   **Rest Between Fries:** Allowing the chicken to rest for 10-15 minutes between the first and second fry helps the internal moisture redistribute, resulting in a juicier interior and a crispier exterior.
*   **Thin Batter is Best:** Unlike some fried chicken recipes, the coating for Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken should be relatively thin, allowing the chicken's skin to become the star of the crispiness.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a showstopper on its own, but traditional Korean side dishes and drinks perfectly complement its rich flavors.

The classic accompaniment for Korean fried chicken is a small bowl of **pickled radishes (chicken-mu)**. Their tangy, refreshing crunch provides a perfect counterpoint to the rich, spicy chicken, cleansing the palate between bites. A simple side of **steamed white rice** is also excellent for soaking up any extra sauce.

For a complete Korean dining experience, pair your Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken with a **cold beer (maekju)**, which is so popular in Korea that the combination has its own portmanteau: "Chimaek" (chicken and maekju). Soju is another traditional alcoholic beverage that pairs wonderfully. Non-alcoholic options include chilled barley tea or a refreshing sparkling water.

Garnish your finished dish with a generous sprinkle of **toasted sesame seeds** and finely chopped **scallions** for added flavor and visual appeal. Some people also enjoy a side of coleslaw or a simple green salad to cut through the richness.

## Variations & FAQs

The beauty of Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken lies in its versatility. Here are some variations and answers to common questions:

**Variations:**
*   **Spice Level:** Easily adjust the heat by increasing or decreasing the amount of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes). For a milder version, you can reduce the gochujang slightly and increase the sugar.
*   **Sweetness:** Experiment with the amount of sugar or corn syrup to achieve your preferred level of sweetness. Honey can also be used for a slightly different flavor profile.
*   **Nutty Flavor:** For a richer, more complex sauce, some recipes include a tablespoon of smooth peanut butter or finely ground peanuts.
*   **Garlic & Ginger Boost:** If you're a fan of strong aromatics, don't hesitate to add a little extra minced garlic or grated ginger to your sauce.
*   **Boneless Chicken:** While wings and drumsticks are traditional, boneless chicken thighs or even chicken breast cutlets work wonderfully, often reducing frying time.
*   **Vegan Version:** Tofu or mushrooms can be battered and double-fried, then tossed in a vegan-friendly Yangnyeom sauce (ensure gochujang is vegan, as some contain fish products).

**FAQs:**
*   **Can I make it less spicy?** Yes! Reduce the gochujang and omit the gochugaru for a milder sauce. You can also add a touch more sugar or corn syrup to balance the heat.
*   **What if I don't have gochujang?** Gochujang is quite unique, and finding a perfect substitute is challenging for authentic flavor. However, in a pinch, a mix of sriracha, miso paste, and a touch of sugar can offer a similar sweet-spicy-umami profile, though it won't be exactly the same.
*   **Can I bake or air fry the chicken?** While you won't get the same deep crispiness as traditional double-frying, an air fryer can produce a decent result. Bake at a high temperature (around 400°F/200°C) for 30-40 minutes, flipping halfway, then air fry at 375°F (190°C) for 15-20 minutes, flipping once. The double-fry technique is truly superior for Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken.
*   **How do I store and reheat leftovers?** Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is best enjoyed fresh. If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days. Reheat in an air fryer at 350°F (175°C) for 8-10 minutes or in a conventional oven at 375°F (190°C) for 15-20 minutes to regain some crispiness. The sauce might become a bit stickier, but the flavor will still be fantastic.

Embarking on the journey to create your own Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a rewarding culinary adventure. With this guide, you have all the tools and tips needed to recreate this incredibly popular and delicious Korean chicken recipe at home. So gather your ingredients, fire up the fryer, and prepare to indulge in the crispy, saucy perfection of homemade Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken!
